problem.summary: SHA1_Final on SG2044 (RVV 1.0 @ VLEN=128) runs entirely in scalar mode: IPC=0.859, test_store_cases=5.0s, 184 lines pure scalar assembly. No RISC-V SHA1 vector assembly exists in OpenSSL — only generic C fallback. HASH_MAKE_STRING consumes 35 scalar instructions per 5-word state.
evidence: perf annotate: SHA1_Final 184 lines, zero v* vector instructions, all scalar
perf stat: IPC=0.859 (latency-bound), L1 miss=1.541% (not memory-bound), branch_miss=8.135%
No sha1-riscv64*.pl exists in crypto/sha/asm/ — only x86, ARM, PPC, etc.
sha_riscv.c provides generic C SHA1, autovectorizer cannot handle SHA1 dependency chains

rootCause.summary: OpenSSL lacks RISC-V vector SHA1 assembly. Build config already has -march=rv64gcv_zvkned but without SHA1 vector assembly, the C compiler cannot auto-vectorize SHA1's complex round dependencies and message scheduling.
performanceFactors: IPC=0.859 (scalar dependency chain limits ILP on 3-wide C920)
HASH_MAKE_STRING: 35 scalar instructions for 5 words (7 per word: lw+3srlw+3sb)

mustPreserve: SHA1 test vectors must pass (NIST CAVP test cases)
Scalar C fallback for non-RVV hardware
Existing SHA-256/SHA-512 vector paths must not regress
FIPS module integrity (SHA1 not in FIPS but libcrypto must stay consistent)
